{"credits": [], "cve_id": null, "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 6.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:H/AT:P/PR:N/UI:N/VC:N/VI:L/VA:L/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-188", "name": "Reliance on Data/Memory Layout"}], "description": "Diesel allows to register custom aggregate SQL functions for SQLite via the `SqliteAggregate` interface.

To store an instance of the custom aggregate processor Diesel relied on the `sqlite3_aggregate_context` function provided by sqlite. This function doesn't provide any guarantees about alignment of the returned allocation, which in turn can lead to problems if the type implementing requires a special alignment, e.g. via a custom `#[align(x)]` attribute on the type implementing this trait. This affects any user of `SqliteAggregate` that registers the custom aggregate function with an SQLite connection, while using a non-standard alignment on the type implementing this trait.

## Mitigation

The preferred mitigation to the outlined problem is to update to a Diesel version 2.3.8 or newer, which includes fixes for the problem.

## Resolution

Diesel now allocates the corresponding memory on Rust side to get a correctly aligned allocation.", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-q8x8-jrhj-fh9p", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-19T19:39:37Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-q8x8-jrhj-fh9p", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-q8x8-jrhj-fh9p"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-05-19T19:39:37Z", "references": ["https://github.com/diesel-rs/diesel/pull/5042", "https://rustsec.org/advisories/RUSTSEC-2026-0137.html", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-q8x8-jrhj-fh9p"], "repository_advisory_url": null, "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/diesel-rs/diesel", "summary": "Diesel: Possible unaligned data access for implementations of `SqliteAggregate`", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-19T19:39:39Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-q8x8-jrhj-fh9p", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "2.3.8", "package": {"ecosystem": "rust", "name": "diesel"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 2.3.8"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Diesel: Possible unaligned data access for implementations of `SqliteAggregate`
